source
The initial step involves initializing the accumulator variables to handle the mathematical operations required for the summation of squares and the product of cubes.
***
We must verify if the input slice is empty to correctly return the default values of zero for the sum and one for the product as specified in the requirements.
***
If the list contains elements, we iterate through each integer to calculate its square and add it to the running total for the first component of the result pair.
***
Simultaneously during the iteration, we compute the cube of each number and multiply it with the running product variable to build the second component.
***
After processing all items in the slice, we construct the final ordered pair using the calculated sum of squares and the computed product of cubes.
***
The function concludes by returning this two-element integer array which represents the final mathematical transformation of the input set.
